notes: Converted from the old chapter extraction schema.
Hidden facts, motives, and faction pressures require living-world re-extraction.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

The seed package describes public battle events (Fang Yuan killing Li Huang, Chen Yi using cause-effect divine tree to teleport enemies). All events are visible to observers on the battlefield. No private knowledge, secret plans, or future-known information is directly supported by the source pointers. The only potentially hidden element is Chen Yi's inheritance from Yuan Lian, but that is stated openly in the text (陈衣 realizes the tree painting is from Yuan Lian's true inheritance). No actor is shown to privately know something others don't. No directly supported knowledge gap exists in the provided paragraphs.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

The seed shows actions (方源击杀厉煌, 冲向毛脚山) but no direct evidence of character's internal motive. Observed behavior is combat-oriented, but inferring motive like '急于攻占毛脚山' or '清除障碍' would be a psychological leap beyond what the seed directly supports. Only behavior is visible; motive is ambiguous.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

insufficient source-backed seed: The seed primarily describes individual combat actions (方源 vs 厉煌, 方源 vs 陈衣, 陈衣 vs 武庸/百足天君/楚度) without establishing any named collective faction/group/clan/family as having a concrete stake. Entities like '中洲一方' are vague labels on individual persons (厉煌, 武庸, 药皇) but no organized faction entity is named or described with collective interests. No family, clan, hall, shop, or organization appears as a named collective actor in the seed facts or relations. Individual-only pressure subjects cannot be used.
IF export filter quarantined 8 legacy old-schema branches.
